subject: soccer moms
one_liner: Fleet turns every minivan in your league into a fulfillment center. Snacks, shin guards, and carpool capacity, routed before the whistle.
brand_voice_notes: Ops-speak applied to youth soccer. Everything is logistics: nothing is a favor, everything is routed, assigned, fulfilled, or reconciled. The company speaks with the confidence of 40 percent household penetration. No exclamation points. The word 'volunteer' appears only in the past tense. Sentimentality is filed under shrinkage.

hero.h1: Every minivan is a fulfillment center.
hero.sub: Fleet coordinates snacks, shin guards, and 7 a.m. carpool capacity across your entire league. Nobody volunteers anymore. The network assigns.

features.title: Distributed Trunk Inventory
features.blurb: Your cargo area is now a micro-fulfillment node. We stock it to par levels overnight. Other families collect from your tailgate between quarters. Eye contact is optional and discouraged.

features.title: Snack Duty Futures
features.blurb: Snack obligations are now a liquid market. Short your Saturday. Hedge the tournament. One mother in Naperville cleared $340 in October and she will not say how.

features.title: The Kickoff Guarantee
features.blurb: Forgotten shin guards reach any field in our coverage area in eleven minutes, handed off by whichever parent the routing engine decided was closest. That is the guarantee. Eleven minutes.

pricing_tiers.name: Orange Slice
pricing_tiers.tagline: For families still pretending this is optional.

pricing_tiers.name: Travel Team
pricing_tiers.tagline: The tier most of your league is already on.

pricing_tiers.name: Tournament Weekend
perks: A dedicated routing strategist named Craig
We talk to the other parents for you

testimonials.quote: I haven't set foot in a grocery store since March. My trunk restocks itself overnight. I've stopped asking how they get in.
testimonials.name: Denise Kowalczyk
testimonials.title: Interim Snack Coordinator, Brookfield Strikers U-9

testimonials.quote: The routing engine assigned me a carpool of children I had never met. We made both games. One of them is ours now, I think.
testimonials.name: Marcus Tran
testimonials.title: Father of Twins on Different Teams in Different Counties

testimonials.quote: I shorted snack duty the week before spring break and cleared enough for goalie camp. The other mothers know it was me. Nobody can prove it.
testimonials.name: Paula Reyes-Whitford
testimonials.title: Treasurer, Sherwood Park Sideline Association (dissolved)

demo_screen_concept: Sideline Command, the dispatch console: Saturday morning rendered as a fulfillment queue. Left panel lists today's matches as order rows (field, kickoff time, status chips: PICKING, IN TRANSIT, DELIVERED TO SIDELINE) filterable by division tabs (U-7, U-9, U-12). Center panel shows trunk inventory for your minivan node with par-level bars and a LOW STOCK alert on Capri Suns, plus a 'Dispatch resupply' button that starts a live 11:00 countdown with status updates. Right panel is the snack-duty futures ticker with BUY and SHORT buttons that update a fake position and P&L in place.

images.rationale: hero skipped: typographic bento-grid style carries the hero with type and live-looking cells; demo assets skipped: Sideline Command is a dispatch console (terminal/map-shaped), no photo slots; full remaining budget on 3 deadpan corporate headshots for the named testimonials

items.prompt: Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of a suburban mother in her early forties, sensible bob haircut, quarter-zip fleece over a collared shirt, faint tired smile of someone who runs logistics for a youth soccer team, studio lighting, plain pale sage-white backdrop with a very subtle green cast like #F4F6F0, muted natural color grade, editorial photograph, one focal subject centered, shoulders up. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of an East Asian father in his late thirties, slightly disheveled, wearing a performance polo shirt, expression of calm exhaustion, the look of a man who drove to two counties before nine in the morning, studio lighting, plain pale sage-white backdrop with a very subtle green cast like #F4F6F0, muted natural color grade, editorial photograph, one focal subject centered, shoulders up. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of a poised Latina woman in her late forties, blazer over an athletic top, reading glasses pushed up into her hair, the composed half-smile of a treasurer who outmaneuvered an entire sideline association, studio lighting, plain pale sage-white backdrop with a very subtle green cast like #F4F6F0, muted natural color grade, editorial photograph, one focal subject centered, shoulders up. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

design_direction.style: Bento-grid sideline logistics console, chalk-line daylight mode. The page is staged as the marketing site of a heavily funded everything-store that has decided youth soccer is a fulfillment problem and prices snack duty by the trunk. A pale chalk-white ground with a faint green cast carries thin white chalk field markings (a center-circle arc behind the hero, a penalty-box corner at the footer) drawn in near-invisible white strokes, so the page reads as a pitch surveyed by an operations team rather than a website. Every section is a bento lattice: flat white cells with 2px pitch-green borders, 14px radii, no shadows of any kind; depth comes entirely from border weight, green-tinted well fills, and spacing. Cells wear routing-label chrome: uppercase mono microcopy in wide tracking (NODE-114 / TRUNK B, ROUTE 6, PAR LEVEL) and thin CSS barcode strips (repeating-linear-gradient, no images) as section dividers, so every panel reads as a parcel that has been through a sorting facility. One pitch green carries the wordmark, headlines' punctuation, all borders, structural labels, and institutional surfaces; one rationed cone orange, the exact orange of a practice cone, is reserved strictly for the single primary CTA per section (ink text on orange fill for contrast), the live dispatch dot, LOW STOCK alerts, and futures upticks, and appears almost nowhere else as fill. Archivo Black sets the wordmark and headlines in heavy grotesque caps with jersey-number confidence; Archivo carries plain, faintly procedural body copy. Every figure that pretends to be fulfillment telemetry (ETAs, SKU counts, par levels, futures prices, tier prices, trunk temperatures) is set in IBM Plex Mono with tabular figures inside green-ruled label wells, so it reads like a routing manifest, not marketing. Motion is warehouse-restrained: the dispatch dot pulses on a 3s cycle, bento cells rise 6px and fade in on scroll with a 40ms stagger, ETA figures tick down digit by digit, the CTA depresses 1px on press, futures prices tick in place with a brief orange flash, all gated behind prefers-reduced-motion. No dark mode, no glass, no blur, no clay, no serifs, no offset brutalist shadows, no postal or stationery motifs, no marquee, no emoji. Reads like a company that files a mother's Saturday under 'route capacity' and lists a forgotten juice box as a stockout event.
design_direction.archetype: E-commerce/logistics marketplace landing, Amazon-adjacent, staged as a sideline dispatch console: slim wordmark nav with a live dispatch dot and a 'Join the network' CTA, split hero with a giant Archivo Black claim left and a bento cluster right (trunk inventory cell with par bars, kickoff ETA cell with mono countdown, snack futures mini-ticker cell), features as three bento cells with routing-label chrome, testimonials as delivery-confirmation cards with headshots in green-ruled ID frames, pricing as three route plans, a cone-orange CTA band, and a handoff to the Sideline Command demo.
design_direction.motion_vocab: dispatch dot pulses on a 3s cycle; bento cells rise 6px and fade in on scroll with 40ms stagger; ETA figures tick down digit by digit; CTA depresses 1px on press; futures prices tick in place with a brief orange flash on change; all gated behind prefers-reduced-motion
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Split hero on a faint chalk center-circle arc: left column carries a kicker chip (SIDELINE LOGISTICS NETWORK), the h1 at clamp(2.6rem, 7vw, 5.2rem) in Archivo Black, the sub in Archivo, and the single orange CTA with ink text; right column is a stacked bento cluster of three live-looking cells (trunk par bars, an 11:00 ETA countdown in IBM Plex Mono, a three-row futures mini-ticker), each with 2px green borders and routing-label microcopy.
